title: (No AI) Anthropic’s Claude Code gets ‘safer’ auto mode
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/STK269_ANTHROPIC_2_A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Anthropic has [launched](https://claude.com/blog/auto-mode) an "auto mode" for [Claude Code](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/860730/anthropic-cowork-feature-ai-agents-claude-code), a new tool that lets AI make permissions-level decisions on users' behalf. The company says the feature offers vibe coders a safer alternative between constant handholding or giving the model dangerous levels of autonomy.

Claude Code is [capable of acting independently](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899430/anthropic-claude-code-cowork-ai-control-computer) on users' behalf, a useful but risky feature as it can also do things users don't want, like deleting files, sending out sensitive data, and executing malicious code or [hidden instructions](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/881574/cline-openclaw-prompt-injection-hack). Auto mode is designed to prevent this, flagging and blocking potentially risky actions before they run and offering the agent a chan …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/900201/anthropic-claude-code-auto-mode)
contentSnippet:
Anthropic has launched an "auto mode" for Claude Code, a new tool that lets AI make permissions-level decisions on users' behalf. The company says the feature offers vibe coders  a safer alternative between constant handholding or giving the model dangerous levels of autonomy. 
Claude Code is capable of acting independently on users' behalf, a useful but risky feature as it can also do things users don't want, like deleting files, sending out sensitive data, and executing malicious code or hidden instructions. Auto mode is designed to prevent this, flagging and blocking potentially risky actions before they run and offering the agent a chan …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Anthropic has launched an "auto mode" for Claude Code, a new tool that lets AI make permissions-level decisions on users' behalf. The company says the feature offers vibe coders a safer alternative between constant handholding or giving the model dangerous levels of autonomy. Claude Code is capable of acting independently on users' behalf, a useful […]

title: (No AI) OpenAI just gave up on Sora and its billion-dollar Disney deal
author: Richard Lawler
content:
![Frame from a video generated by Sora 2 showing a cowboy riding a horse on top of another horse](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/09/ai-label-1.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

A frame from a Sora 2-generated video. | Image: OpenAI

On Tuesday afternoon, OpenAI announced "We're saying goodbye to Sora," the video generation tool that it launched [at the end of 2024](https://www.theverge.com/2024/12/9/24317092/openai-sora-text-to-video-ai-launch), and centered in a massive licensing deal with Disney only a few months ago. _[The Wall Street Journal](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/digital/openai-shutting-down-sora-ai-video-app-1236546187/)_ reported the move earlier, saying that OpenAI boss Sam Altman had informed staff that both the TikTok-like Sora app and API access for developers would be discontinued, with no plans to roll the feature into ChatGPT as had previously been [rumored](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/openai-plans-launch-sora-video-ai-chatgpt-strategy-shift?rc=v4bmzs).

According to _[The Hollywood Reporter](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/digital/openai-shutting-down-sora-ai-video-app-1236546187/)_, as a result, the deal Disney announced in December, [saying it would invest $1 billion in OpenAI](https://www.theverge.com/news/842348/disney-openai-sora-chatgpt-images), license its characters for use w …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899850/openai-sora-ai-chatgpt)
contentSnippet:
A frame from a Sora 2-generated video. | Image: OpenAI	

On Tuesday afternoon, OpenAI announced "We're saying goodbye to Sora," the video generation tool that it launched at the end of 2024, and centered in a massive licensing deal with Disney only a few months ago. The Wall Street Journal reported the move earlier, saying that OpenAI boss Sam Altman had informed staff that both the TikTok-like Sora app and API access for developers would be discontinued, with no plans to roll the feature into ChatGPT as had previously been rumored.
According to The Hollywood Reporter, as a result, the deal Disney announced in December, saying it would invest $1 billion in OpenAI, license its characters for use w …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The Wall Street Journal reported the move earlier, saying that OpenAI boss Sam Altman had informed staff that both […]

creator: Lauren Goode
title: (No AI) Arm’s CEO Insists the Market Needs His New CPU. It Could Piss Everyone Off

content: Arm just confirmed the rumors: It’s producing its own chip for the first time. CEO Rene Haas explains why this won’t alienate the many chipmakers who license the company’s designs.
